title: ORBridge
summary: Defines an abstract termwise bridge between a formal Juhl right-hand side and an OR right-hand side.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Defines an abstract termwise bridge between a formal Juhl right-hand side and an OR right-hand side.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Juhlformula/Juhlcombinatorics/ORBridge.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A bridge between one Juhl composition term and one OR/poly-laplacian term. This is intentionally abstract in the first version. Later versions can replace orExpr by a concrete OR operator object and keep this as the formal shadow. Its mathematical data are: juhl Coeff: ℚ; juhl Expr: Formal Expr; or Coeff: ℚ; or Expr: Formal Expr; coeff eq: juhl Coeff equals or Coeff; expr eq: Formal Eq juhl Expr or Expr.
A finite bridge between a formal Juhl RHS and an OR RHS. Its mathematical data are: juhl RHS: Formal Juhl RHS; or RHS: Formal Juhl RHS; bridge eq: Formal Eq juhl RHS.to Expr or RHS.to Expr.

The identity bridge from a RHS to itself. In explicit terms, for r of type formal juhl rhs, its defining expression is juhl RHS : equals R or RHS : equals R bridge eq : equals Formal Eq.refl R.to Expr.
Placeholder for later conversion from actual OR data to a formal RHS. Its mathematical data are: order: the natural numbers; rhs: Formal Juhl RHS.

references.locator: Formal Juhl-type formula and OR bridge
references.note: Primary source for the intended curved OR–Juhl identity.
Source for the Juhl side of the formal syntax.

limitations: This file establishes syntax, aggregate imports, or an abstract bridge. It must not be read as a derivation of the corresponding geometric theorem from concrete tensor and analytic data.
